revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Goshoyama Kofun]][[ja:御所山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Goshoyama Kofun | native_name =御所山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Goshoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.758511|N|130.980744|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en|Kanda, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206876|lt=Kanda, Fukuoka|1=Kanda, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|45|30.64|N|130|58|50.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Goshoyama Kofun'''|御所山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Yohara neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en|Kanda, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206876|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District|1=Miyako District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936, with the area under protection expanded in 2012, 2020 and 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2630|title=御所山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Along with the [[Ishizukayama Kofun]] (also located in Kanda) it is one of the largest Kofuns in the {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|qid=Q1047134|lt=Buzen region|1=Buzen Province}}, and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-late Kofun period..
==Overview== The Goshoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a low hill overlooking the Gulf of Suo on the [[Seto Inland Sea]] in eastern Fukuoka Prefecture. It is known that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in 1820, but a proper [[archaeological excavation]]s was not conducted until 2007 to 2015. The tumulus retains its original appearance, and the five-meter wide moat surrounding it is filled with water. The total length of the tumulus is 119 meters; the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 82 meters, and the diameter of the circular posterior portion is 73 meters, and there are cutouts on both sides of the constriction between the anterior and posterior portions. The tumulus was built in three stages, and there are ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The total length including the surrounding embankment and moat is about 140 meters. The horizontal-entry stone burial chamber in the posterior circular part has a narrow passageway at the entrance, orientated to the north. The burial chamber is 4.7 by three meters, with stone barrier dividing the floors into several sections for multiple burials. The inner surface was painted with vermillion pigment. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found inside the burial chamber included a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, ''[[magatama]]'', pipe beads, and gilt-bronze horse fittings, possibly originating from the Asian mainland. These artifacts are now in the collection of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}. It is believed that the tumulus was constructed in the late 5th century, based on the structure, grave goods, and style of ''haniwa''. It is speculated from the size of the tumulus that this was the graves of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient Buzen Province. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kanda Station (Fukuoka) |en|Kanda Station (Fukuoka) |qid=Q5360826|lt=Kanda Station|1=Kanda Station (Fukuoka)}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The top of the ''kofun'' is now occupied by a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanda.lg.jp/_3861/_3911/_3921.html Kanda town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13199 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}
